message.content:
@startuml
skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center
autonumber "<b>[0]"

actor client
participant gateway
box "developer-service" #LightBlue2
  participant developer_service as "developer-service\n(REST API)"
end box
database devs_db as "devs-db"
queue broker as "broker"
package planning {
    rectangle planner_api <<interface>> as planapi "#WhiteSmoke-#DarkGreen;line:green;text:black;##green;fontSize=14;\nplanning-api\ngRPC Service" 
}
rectangle planner_svc as "planner-svc\n(gRPC Server)" #YellowOrange
database plansdb as "plans-db"


client ->> gateway : POST /developers/{id}/plan \ndevId={...}, name="..." , ...
gateway --> client : HTTP/1.1 5xx Internal Error 

activate gateway
alt dev not found or other error in REST call to create DevPlan
   activate developer_service  
      note over developer_service,gateway: If any of these conditions are met:\n - Developer does NOT exist.\n - Plan already exists for this user.\nThen return an appropriate status code like **HTTP 4XX** . The exact one depends on your business logic but typically would be something along lines below 👇🏻   
         alt No such entity/resource was located during processing request body parameters sent by caller via URL path/query string/header etc., then respond back wth corresponding failure response containing suitable message explaining why operation couldnt complete successfully due lack required information being provided upfront before attempting execution itself! For example if trying update record whose primary key value wasn't specified correctly inside payload JSON object passed through PUT/PATCH methods instead using GET which doesn’t require specifying target resource identifier explicitly since it’s implied from endpoint URI pattern matching rules defined at routing layer level within framework used here namely Spring Boot MVC based architecture style following best practices recommended officially docs available online freely accessible anywhere anytime without restrictions whatsoever except maybe bandwidth limitations imposed locally
